facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-centered atmosphere over sterile efficiency
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward inviting, personal interface—softer colors, rounded forms, conversational microcopy. Suggests CRM should feel like a trusted colleague, not corporate software.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp, professional distance—cooler palettes, sharp geometry, minimal prose. Suggests CRM should project competence and clarity over warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable client overviews (names, status, last contact) in card layouts over dense tabular spreadsheets
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values visual hierarchy and quick pattern recognition over comprehensive data density; likely prefers dashboard-style information architecture with progressive disclosure
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes comprehensive data access and comparison; may prefer traditional table/grid layouts where all fields are simultaneously visible; values analytical depth over visual simplicity

evidence.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-centered atmosphere over sterile efficiency
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward inviting, personal interface—softer colors, rounded forms, conversational microcopy. Suggests CRM should feel like a trusted colleague, not corporate software.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, gentle interfaces that feel less corporate and more human
facade.axisTargeted: Roundness vs sharpness + depth via shadow
facade.label: Soft corners and shadows
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward warm, welcoming aesthetic with rounded buttons/cards and subtle drop shadows—softer than minimal flat design
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer crisp, modern edges and flat/bold contrast—either clean minimalism or strong geometric definition

facade.hypothesis: A friendly, conversational voice will feel more approachable to solo freelancers than corporate language
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, conversational microcopy; likely wants personality in empty states, confirmations, and error messages; may value humor and relatability over formal polish
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers professional, straightforward language; likely wants a business-focused tone; may see friendliness as unprofessional or distracting in a work tool

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, immediate feedback when actions happen (no loading states)
facade.axisTargeted: Snappy instant vs. smooth animated transitions
facade.label: Instant updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values responsiveness and zero-latency feel; wants clicks to feel immediate and confirm instantly
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers deliberate pacing with visible transitions; may want animations that give time to process or add polish

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent client access on the left while viewing detailed project cards in the main area—reducing clicks to switch contexts.
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick client switching and prefers vertical hierarchy. Likely wants persistent navigation + modular content views.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based navigation, flatter hierarchies, or list-heavy layouts. Context-switching via tabs or full-page changes feels natural to them.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive coach
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers numbers/metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries and stories—reveals how they track success and make decisions
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals patience level and preference for responsiveness in a fast-paced freelance workflow

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to gentle, diffused visual hierarchy (subtle depth cues) than hard borders and stark contrast, reinforcing the 'trusted colleague' atmosphere over corporate rigidity
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: CRM interface should use low-contrast borders, diffused edges, gentle elevation effects, and muted color transitions to feel approachable and less demanding
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers may prefer clarity through contrast and defined structure—crisp borders, bold hierarchy, high-contrast elements to quickly scan client data and priorities
